title: Leionema montanum
taxonomy: Leionema montanum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of Australia. Leionema montanum is a member of the genus Leionema, which consists of several species of shrubs. The plant is also known as Mountain Lemon-myrtle or Lemon-scented Bush. It is a unique and distinctive species within the Rutaceae family.

intro: Leionema montanum, also known as Mountain Raisin, is a small, shrubby plant belonging to the Rutaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian herbal medicine for its health benefits, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been reported to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and protect against various infections. Bioactive constituents of Leionema montanum include volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Mountain Raisin include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal problems.
